2606.25306.title: Physics Question Scene Graph: Fine-grained Evaluation of Physical Plausibility in Text-to-Video Generation
2606.25306.paper_summary: Video generation models are increasingly capable of producing realistic videos, but they still struggle to generate videos that follow basic physical laws. Compounding this is a lack of reliable granular evaluation methods for localizing and specifying physical law violations in videos. We address this by introducing Physics Question Scene Graph (PQSG), a hierarchical question-based evaluation pipeline. PQSG evaluates generated videos by checking their faithfulness to a prompt across objects, actions, and adherence to physical laws using a graph-based hierarchy of questions generated by a vision-language model (VLM), guided by high-quality in-context examples. By representing questions as a graph, PQSG introduces logical dependencies within questions, ensuring that each query is contextually valid. Moreover, PQSG provides granular assessments of which qualities of the video violate physical plausibility constraints. We validate PQSG by creating FinePhyEval, a dataset with physics-based prompts and corresponding generated videos from diverse state-of-the-art video generation models (Sora 2, Veo 3, and Wan 2.1), with each video annotated across multiple categories by humans. Using FinePhyEval, we measure the correlation between PQSG's fine-grained scores and human judgments, showing higher overall correlations than prior work. We also find that PQSG ranks closed-source models higher than Wan 2.1 on physical realism. Lastly, we show that the annotations we provide in FinePhyEval can also be used for subtask evaluation: we benchmark two strong VLMs on generating and answering questions, finding that while models can create human-like questions, they still fall short of human performance in answering them.
2606.25306.paper_summary_zh: 視頻生成模型越來越能夠生成逼真的視頻，但它們仍然難以生成遵循基本物理法則的視頻。更糟糕的是，缺乏可靠的細粒度評估方法來定位和具體說明視頻中的物理法則違反情況。我們通過引入物理問題場景圖（PQSG）來解決這個問題，這是一個基於問題的分層評估管道。PQSG 通過檢查生成視頻在物體、動作和遵循物理法則方面對提示的忠實度來評估生成的視頻，使用由視覺-語言模型（VLM）生成的基於圖的問題層次結構，並由高質量的上下文示例指導。通過將問題表示為圖，PQSG 在問題內引入邏輯依賴性，確保每個查詢在上下文中都是有效的。此外，PQSG 提供了對視頻中哪些特質違反物理合理性約束的細粒度評估。我們通過創建 FinePhyEval 來驗證 PQSG，這是一個包含基於物理的提示和來自各種最先進視頻生成模型（Sora 2、Veo 3 和 Wan 2.1）對應生成視頻的數據集，每個視頻都由人類在多個類別中進行註釋。使用 FinePhyEval，我們測量 PQSG 的細粒度分數與人類判斷之間的相關性，顯示出比先前工作更高的整體相關性。我們還發現 PQSG 在物理真實性方面將封閉源模型的排名高於 Wan 2.1。最後，我們展示了我們在 FinePhyEval 中提供的註釋也可以用於子任務評估：我們對兩個強大的 VLM 在生成和回答問題方面進行基準測試，發現儘管模型可以創建類似人類的問題，但在回答問題方面仍然不及人類表現。
2606.25306.author: Atin Pothiraj et.al.
2606.25306.authors: Atin Pothiraj, Jaemin Cho, Yue Zhang, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Mohit Bansal
